name: hostname
title: hostname
summary: utility to set/show the host name or domain name
description:
utility to set/show the host name or domain name
This package provides commands which can be used to display the system's
DNS name, and to display or set its hostname or NIS domain name.

commands.name: hostname
commands.description: show or set the system's host name

arguments.description: all FQDNs of the machine. This option enumerates all configured network ad-
arguments.raw: -A  all FQDNs of the machine. This option enumerates all configured network ad-

arguments.description: Display the alias name of the host (if used). This option is deprecated and should not
arguments.raw: -a  Display the alias name of the host (if used). This option is deprecated and should not

arguments.description: Display the alias name of the host (if used). This option is deprecated and should not
arguments.raw: --alias  Display the alias name of the host (if used). This option is deprecated and should not

arguments.description: all FQDNs of the machine. This option enumerates all configured network ad-
arguments.raw: --all-fqdns  all FQDNs of the machine. This option enumerates all configured network ad-

arguments.description: Display all network addresses of the host. This option enumerates all configured ad-
arguments.raw: --all-ip-addresses  Display all network addresses of the host. This option enumerates all configured ad-

arguments.description: Always set a hostname; this allows the file specified by -F to be non-existent or
arguments.raw: -b  Always set a hostname; this allows the file specified by -F to be non-existent or

arguments.description: Always set a hostname; this allows the file specified by -F to be non-existent or
arguments.raw: --boot  Always set a hostname; this allows the file specified by -F to be non-existent or

arguments.description: the name of the DNS domain. Don't use the command domainname to get the DNS

arguments.description: the name of the DNS domain. Don't use the command domainname to get the DNS
arguments.raw: --domain  the name of the DNS domain. Don't use the command domainname to get the DNS

arguments.description: the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name). A FQDN consists of a short host name
arguments.raw: -f  the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name). A FQDN consists of a short host name

arguments.description: the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name). A FQDN consists of a short host name
arguments.raw: --fqdn  the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name). A FQDN consists of a short host name

arguments.description: Print a usage message and exit.
arguments.raw: -h  Print a usage message and exit.

arguments.description: Print a usage message and exit.
arguments.raw: --help  Print a usage message and exit.

arguments.description: Display all network addresses of the host. This option enumerates all configured ad-
arguments.raw: -I  Display all network addresses of the host. This option enumerates all configured ad-

arguments.description: Display the network address(es) of the host name. Note that this works only if the host
arguments.raw: -i  Display the network address(es) of the host name. Note that this works only if the host

arguments.description: Display the network address(es) of the host name. Note that this works only if the host
arguments.raw: --ip-address  Display the network address(es) of the host name. Note that this works only if the host

arguments.description: the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name). A FQDN consists of a short host name
arguments.raw: --long  the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name). A FQDN consists of a short host name

arguments.description: Display the NIS domain name. If a parameter is given (or --file name ) then root can
arguments.raw: --nis  Display the NIS domain name. If a parameter is given (or --file name ) then root can

arguments.description: Display the short host name. This is the host name cut at the first dot.
arguments.raw: -s  Display the short host name. This is the host name cut at the first dot.

arguments.description: Display the short host name. This is the host name cut at the first dot.
arguments.raw: --short  Display the short host name. This is the host name cut at the first dot.

arguments.description: Print version information on standard output and exit successfully.

arguments.description: Print version information on standard output and exit successfully.
arguments.raw: --version  Print version information on standard output and exit successfully.

commands.name: dnsdomainname
commands.description: show the system's DNS domain name

commands.name: domainname
commands.description: show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name

commands.name: nisdomainname
commands.description: show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name

commands.name: ypdomainname
commands.description: show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use hostname because utility to set/show the host name or domain name.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use hostname as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hostname  [-a|--alias]  [-d|--domain]  [-f|--fqdn|--long]  [-A|--all-fqdns]  [-i|--ip-address]` as documented upstream for hostname.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hostname [-b|--boot] [-F|--file filename] [hostname]` as documented upstream for hostname.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hostname [-h|--help] [-V|--version]` as documented upstream for hostname.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair hostname with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hostname --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: hostname - show or set the system's host name domainname - show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name ypdomainname - show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name nisdomainname - show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name dnsdomainname - show the system's DNS domain name
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install hostname. utility to set/show the host name or domain name.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: utility to set/show the host name or domain name.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: hostname - show or set the system's host name domainname - show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name ypdomainname - show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name nisdomainname - show or set the system's NIS/YP domain name dnsdomainname - show the system's DNS domain name.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install hostname
Install with: sudo apt install hostname
